claim: Threshold-only detection exhibits a frame-level tradeoff
metric: empirical_pd / false_alarm_per_frame at low vs high threshold
interpretation: lowering the threshold buys detections at the cost of heavy clutter

claim: Kalman tracking recovers trajectories but leaves false tracks
metric: false tracks over 4 days at -5..6 dB (stage-08 own labels / strict+windowable evaluation denominator)
interpretation: temporal association restores trajectories yet clutter still forms false tracks. The two counts are DIFFERENT populations: stage 08 labels every confirmed track (purity >= 0.5), whereas the filters are evaluated on the stricter, windowable subset (purity >= 0.8 and long enough to window). All filter metrics in this package use the second denominator.

claim: Hand physics scoring is strong and interpretable
interpretation: transparent rule-based scoring removes a large share of false tracks

claim: Empirical marginal ADS-B priors are discriminative but insufficient
interpretation: marginal priors alone do not beat hand physics

claim: Noise-matched deterministic sequence autoencoders are the strongest method
metric: pooled false reduction / true retention (4 days)

claim: The VAE does not beat the deterministic autoencoders
metric: mean VAE false reduction vs stage-12.5
interpretation: probabilistic latent model matches but does not exceed stage 12.5

claim: Diffusion helps regularization/gap filling but not primary filtering
metric: mean gap-fill improvement over linear interpolation
interpretation: useful as a denoiser/regularizer; secondary as a classifier
the single-day limitation from stage 16 is closed
sys.executable, calibration protection, undefined-cell handling, ignore rules
